scheme_name: Saraswati Sadhana Yojana to Socially and Educationally Backward Girls studying in Class 9th
page_title: Saraswati Sadhana Yojana to Socially and Educationally Backward Girls studying in Class 9th

details: The "Saraswati Sadhana Yojana to Socially and Educationally Backward Girls studying in Class 9" is a welfare scheme by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department of Gujarat. The scheme offers free bicycles to girls enrolled in Class 9 in government and grant-in-aided schools, facilitating easier access to education and encouraging regular school attendance. This physical aid helps in improving educational participation and empowering female students from Socially and Educationally Backward classes.
benefits: Free bicycle provided to each eligible girl student.
eligibility: The applicant must be a permanent resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must be a female student.
The applicant must be studying in Class 9th.
The applicant must be enrolled in a government or grant-in-aided school in Gujarat.
The applicant must belong to the Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC).
The applicant's family income must not exceed ₹6,00,000 per annum.

online: Click on the “Register” button on the homepage.
You will be redirected to the registration form page.
Fill in the required details such as: Name, Email ID, Mobile Number, Password, etc.
Submit the registration form to create your account.
Select the suitable login option (e.g., Citizen Login).
You will be redirected to the login form page.
Enter your registered Email ID/Mobile Number and Password.
Click Login to access your account.
User profile will appear showing all previously taken services. Click on “Request a New Service” or the relevant option.
You will be redirected to a list of services. Select the desired service from the list. Click “Continue to Service”.
A form will open—fill in all the required information. (Optional) Click to auto-fill details from your profile.
Click “Next” after completing each section. Upload/attach the necessary documents or import them via DigiLocker.
Review all information and click “Submit” to complete the application.

faqs.question: What is the primary objective of this scheme?
faqs.answer: The primary objective of the scheme is to encourage education among Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC) girls by providing them with free bicycles, thereby reducing school dropouts and promoting regular school attendance.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme is specifically designed for girl students who are currently studying in Class 9 in government or grant-in-aided schools and belong to the Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC) with a family income not exceeding ₹6,00,000/- per annum.

faqs.question: What type of benefit does the scheme provide to the beneficiaries?
faqs.answer: Under this scheme, eligible girl students are provided with a free bicycle as a form of physical assistance to support their educational journey and help them travel to school conveniently.

faqs.question: What is the rationale behind providing bicycles to Class 9 girls under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The provision of bicycles is intended to ease transportation challenges faced by girls from the Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC), thus reducing barriers to education and encouraging continued enrollment and attendance in secondary education.

faqs.question: Is there any income limit that determines the eligibility of an applicant for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme sets a maximum family income limit of ₹6,00,000/- per annum, and only applicants from families with income equal to or below this threshold are eligible to apply.

faqs.question: Can the application for the Saraswati Sadhana Yojana be submitted online?
faqs.answer: Yes, applicants have the convenience of submitting their application online through the official Digital Gujarat portal .

faqs.question: Can male students apply for benefits under this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, male students are not eligible to apply for this scheme, as it is exclusively designed for female students as part of the state’s initiative to promote girls’ education.

faqs.question: Is submission of a caste certificate mandatory when applying for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, applicants are required to submit a valid caste certificate as one of the supporting documents to establish their eligibility under the Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC).

faqs.question: Is the scheme applicable across all regions of Gujarat?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme is implemented throughout the state of Gujarat and is applicable to all eligible applicants residing in any district or taluka within the state.

faqs.question: Whom should applicants contact if they need more information or assistance regarding the scheme?
faqs.answer: For further assistance or information, applicants are advised to visit the Digital Gujarat portal.
